title: pterolepis litoralis
intro: Pterolepis litoralis is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, commonly known as the heath family, native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pterolepis litoralis include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation, and promoting healing. In herbal preparations, Pterolepis litoralis is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
